lessons.title: Create a Simulated VLAN
lessons.meaning: Creates or enters VLAN 31 inside simulated configuration mode.
when_to_use: Create an approved VLAN before assigning access ports
Follow a documented VLAN number and name
lessons.goal: Create VLAN 31 named FINANCE in the simulated switch.
good_output: VLAN 31 is active and named FINANCE
lessons.safety_warning: Confirm VLAN number, name, scope, and approval before creating it. Simulation only.
lessons.practice_task: Enter the VLAN command in the simulated configuration lesson.
lessons.final_summary: Create only the approved VLAN, then verify it exists before assigning a port.

lessons.title: Configure a Simulated Trunk
lessons.meaning: Sets a simulated switch port to trunk mode for multiple VLANs.
when_to_use: Connect an approved switch, AP, or virtualization host
Carry only the documented allowed VLANs
lessons.goal: Prepare Gi1/0/48 as an approved trunk.
good_output: Allowed VLAN list matches the design
lessons.safety_warning: A trunk change can affect many services. Confirm the remote device, allowed VLANs, and change approval.
lessons.practice_task: Select trunk mode and verify the allowed VLAN list.
lessons.final_summary: Trunks carry multiple VLANs and require exact allowed-list verification.
lessons.hint: Use the switchport command that changes a port to trunk mode.

lessons.title: Add an Interface Description
lessons.meaning: Adds an operational label to a simulated interface.
when_to_use: Document the endpoint or circuit owner
good_output: description Finance-PC appears on the correct interface
bad_output: Description does not match the asset record
lessons.safety_warning: Descriptions are low impact but must not expose sensitive personal information.
lessons.practice_task: Add the approved description and verify the exact port.
lessons.final_summary: Good descriptions connect device records to the physical interface.
lessons.hint: Enter the description text exactly as shown.

lessons.title: Use Shutdown Safely
lessons.meaning: Changes a simulated interface administrative state; shutdown disables it and no shutdown enables it.
when_to_use: Restore an approved interface after checking the cause
Understand the impact of an administrative state
lessons.goal: Enable the approved simulated interface after verification.
good_output: Interface is no longer administratively down
Physical and protocol state match the test result
bad_output: Link still fails for a physical reason
lessons.safety_warning: Shutdown and no shutdown affect service. Confirm the port, approval, and maintenance window first.
lessons.practice_task: Choose the enabling command only after the port identity is confirmed.
lessons.final_summary: No shutdown is a change, not a diagnosis. Verify the cause before using it.
lessons.practice_output: Gi1/0/24  connected  VLAN 31  administrative state up
lessons.hint: The enabling command is no shutdown.

lessons.title: Save a Simulated Configuration
lessons.meaning: Saves the simulated running configuration for the next simulated boot.
when_to_use: Save an approved and verified change
Keep running and startup state aligned
lessons.goal: Save only after verification is complete.
good_output: Expected change is present in startup configuration
rollback_commands: Do not save an unverified change; restore the approved configuration first
lessons.safety_warning: Never save a change just because the command sequence completed. Verify first and follow the change process.
lessons.practice_task: Choose the save command at the end of an approved workflow.
lessons.final_summary: Saving makes the simulated change persist; persistence should be deliberate.
lessons.practice_output:
Building configuration...
[OK] Simulated startup configuration saved.
lessons.hint: Use the Cisco save command only after verification.

lessons.title: Verify the Simulated Change
lessons.meaning: Checks the active simulated interface configuration against the intended change.
when_to_use: Confirm the exact port, VLAN, description, and state
lessons.goal: Prove Gi1/0/24 matches the approved Finance-PC change.
good_output: Correct description, mode, VLAN, and state
rollback_commands: Stop and compare the approved configuration before making another change
lessons.safety_warning: Verification is read-only. Do not skip it before saving or closing the ticket.
lessons.practice_task: Use the verification command and compare each required field.
lessons.final_summary: A configuration is not complete until the result is verified and explainable.
lessons.practice_output:
interface Gi1/0/24
 description Finance-PC
 switchport access vlan 31
 no shutdown
lessons.hint: Start with show running-config interface and the correct port.

lessons.title: Roll Back the Simulated Port
lessons.meaning: Restores a simulated interface to its default configuration.
when_to_use: Return the interface to a documented baseline
lessons.goal: Return Gi1/0/24 to its simulated baseline after the exercise.
good_output: Custom description and access settings are removed
rollback_commands: Restore the approved configuration from the change record, not memory
lessons.safety_warning: Default interface can remove important production settings. It is permitted here only because this is a simulation.
lessons.practice_task: Use the rollback command and verify the baseline.
lessons.final_summary: Rollback is part of safe configuration, not an afterthought.
lessons.practice_output: Gi1/0/24 returned to simulated default baseline.
lessons.hint: Use default interface with the exact lab port.

lessons.title: Configuration Safety Gate
lessons.meaning: Uses a read-only check to confirm scope before a simulated configuration change.
when_to_use: Confirm identity, current state, approval, backup, and rollback
Decide whether a configuration action should proceed
lessons.goal: Pass the safety gate before changing a simulated device.
good_output: Approved change and rollback are documented
bad_output: Attempt to use reload, erase, or shutdown as a shortcut
rollback_commands: Pause the change and escalate for clarification
lessons.safety_warning: A safe technician can stop. Never use a destructive command to discover what a device is doing.
lessons.practice_task: Choose the read-only safety checks before proceeding.
lessons.final_summary: The safest configuration habit is check, approve, change, verify, and rollback.
lessons.practice_output: Safety gate passed. Simulated change may proceed.
lessons.hint: Choose a read-only show command first.
